question: The plot shows CO₂ emissions vs. temperature anomaly. Which statement is correct according to the chart data?

options.A: Temperature anomaly rises with CO₂ emissions up to a point, then declines slightly at the highest emission levels.
options.B: There is no relationship at all between CO₂ emissions and temperature anomaly.
options.C: The points form two completely separate groups with no overlap.
options.D: Temperature anomaly steadily decreases as CO₂ emissions increase across the full range.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the x-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
